nodes.label: Sarah Chen

nodes.label: Linh Pham

nodes.label: Priya Nair
nodes.content: Analyst

nodes.label: Project Falcon — retail market entry (VN)
nodes.content: Market-entry study for a regional retail client entering Vietnam.

nodes.label: Project Harbor — fintech due diligence
nodes.content: Buy-side DD on a SEA payments startup.

nodes.label: Client research brief prompt
nodes.content: You are a strategy analyst. Given a company name and industry, produce: (1) 5-bullet company overview, (2) top 3 competitors with revenue estimates, (3) three risks, (4) two growth levers. Cite a source for every number. Only use sources published after 2024.
nodes.context: Minh's team iterated 6 times; the post-2024 source constraint fixed stale-market-size errors. Works best with GPT-4.1.
nodes.author: Minh Le

nodes.label: Proposal first-draft prompt
nodes.content: Draft a consulting proposal using template v3: situation, approach, workplan (3 phases), team, fees placeholder. Tone: direct, no superlatives. Input: discovery-call notes.
nodes.context: Cuts proposal drafting from 4h to 40min. Requires discovery notes as input or it invents scope.
nodes.author: Minh Le

nodes.label: Expert interview synthesis prompt
nodes.content: Given a raw interview transcript, extract: key claims (with confidence), contradictions with prior interviews, follow-up questions. Output as a table.
nodes.context: Built on Project Harbor; catches contradictions humans miss across 10+ interviews.
nodes.author: Sarah Chen

nodes.label: Rapid SWOT generator
nodes.content: Generate a SWOT for {company} grounded ONLY in the attached research pack. Flag any cell where evidence is weak.
nodes.context: The 'grounded only' clause stops hallucinated strengths. Weak-evidence flags drive follow-up research.
nodes.author: Priya Nair

nodes.label: Fast-track deliverable workflow
nodes.content: For deliverables under $10k: (1) draft with AI using approved prompts, (2) author self-review against checklist, (3) send directly to client same day. Senior review skipped to hit turnaround targets.
nodes.context: Introduced to win small engagements on speed. Saves ~1 day per deliverable.
nodes.author: David Osei

nodes.label: DD data-pack assembly workflow
nodes.content: (1) Pull registry filings, (2) run financials through the extraction agent, (3) cross-check revenue vs bank statements, (4) log anomalies in the red-flag sheet.
nodes.context: Step 3 added after Project Harbor found a 20% revenue overstatement.
nodes.author: Sarah Chen

nodes.label: Client deliverable QA workflow
nodes.content: (1) AI draft, (2) fact-check pass with citations verified, (3) senior consultant review, (4) partner sign-off for >$50k engagements.
nodes.context: The baseline QA path for standard deliverables.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: Financials extraction agent config
nodes.content: Model: gpt-4.1. System: extract P&L lines from uploaded statements into the standard schema; refuse to guess missing values; output JSON. Temperature 0.
nodes.context: Refuse-to-guess instruction added after it fabricated a depreciation line on Harbor.
nodes.author: Sarah Chen

nodes.label: Models hallucinate VN registry numbers
nodes.content: GPT and Claude both invent plausible-looking Vietnamese company registration numbers. Always verify against the National Business Registration Portal.
nodes.context: Cost us a rework cycle on Falcon when a cited registration number didn't exist.
nodes.author: Minh Le

nodes.label: Constrain market sizing to post-2024 sources
nodes.content: Unconstrained prompts pull pre-COVID market reports and overstate growth. Add an explicit source-date constraint.
nodes.context: Root cause of the stale numbers in early Falcon drafts; fixed by the constraint now baked into pr-client-research.
nodes.author: Minh Le

nodes.label: VN→EN translation flattens tone
nodes.content: Machine translation of client interviews loses hedging and politeness cues that matter for reading commitment levels. Have a bilingual reviewer spot-check key quotes.
nodes.context: Misread a lukewarm partner as enthusiastic on Falcon; caught in review.
nodes.author: Linh Pham

nodes.label: Extraction agent fails silently past ~50 pages
nodes.content: Beyond ~50 pages the extraction agent starts skipping statements without warning. Chunk uploads to 30 pages.
nodes.context: Found during Harbor when Q3 statements vanished from output.
nodes.author: David Osei

nodes.label: No real client names in stored prompts
nodes.content: Prompts saved to the firm library must use placeholders ({company}, {client}) — never real client names or confidential figures.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: Every client-facing number needs a cited source
nodes.content: Any figure in a client deliverable must carry a verifiable citation. AI-generated numbers without sources are treated as unverified.
nodes.context: Adopted after two near-miss factual errors in 2025.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: DD uses approved data sources only
nodes.content: Due-diligence work may only rely on the approved source list (registry portals, audited statements, licensed databases).
nodes.author: Sarah Chen

nodes.label: GPT-4.1 is the firm default drafting model
nodes.content: Standardized on GPT-4.1 for drafting (March 2026) after bake-off vs three alternatives on proposal quality.
nodes.context: Consistency of outputs across teams; revisit quarterly.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: Proposal template v3 is mandatory
nodes.content: All proposals use template v3 structure. Older templates retired.
nodes.context: Clients compared inconsistent proposals from two teams in 2025 — embarrassing.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: AI use disclosed in engagement letters
nodes.content: Engagement letters state that AI tools assist drafting and analysis under human review.
nodes.context: Client trust and legal advice both pointed the same way.
nodes.author: An Tran

nodes.label: falcon-research chat (Claude, 2026-06-30)
nodes.content: Archived Claude conversation in which Minh iterated the client research prompt and hit the registry-number hallucination.
nodes.author: Minh Le
